perturbation.original_text: Distributor will pay WGT for each Product Distributor orders an amount equal to WGT's then current WatchGuard Price List in effect on the date of receipt by WGT of Distributor's order, subject to the applicable discount set forth in Exhibit A. WGT may, from time to time, change its WatchGuard Price List, provided that any such change will not be effective under this Agreement unless and until the expiration of forty-five (45) days after WGT gives Distributor written notices of the change.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will pay WGT for each Product Distributor orders an amount equal to WGT's then current WatchGuard Price List in effect on the date of receipt by WGT of Distributor's order, subject to the applicable discount set forth in Exhibit A. WGT may change its WatchGuard Price List at any time, and such change will be effective immediately upon notice to Distributor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires WGT to provide a 45-day notice before changes to the price list become effective. The modified text allows WGT to change the price list immediately upon notice. This creates a contradiction because Section 3.2 now allows for both a delayed and immediate price change, leading to disputes over which price list applies at the time of order.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor will pay WGT for each Product Distributor orders an amount equal to WGT's then current WatchGuard Price List in effect on the date of receipt by WGT of Distributor's order, subject to the applicable discount set forth in Exhibit A. WGT may change its WatchGuard Price List at any time, and such change will be effective immediately upon notice to Distributor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement by allowing WGT to change the price list immediately upon notice, while the original text requires a 45-day notice period. The contradicted text (which is incidentally the same as the changed text) reiterates the immediate change. This creates a direct conflict because both versions of the text cannot be simultaneously true. The agreement is contradictory on whether a price change occurs immediately or after 45 days, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes, satisfying the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor will use its best efforts aggressively to develop sales of the Product in the Territory. In furtherance thereof, Distributor will: (a) keep on hand a reasonable inventory of the Product sufficient to allow for prompt delivery of the Product to resellers; (b) establish a program to market the Product, including, but not limited to, participating regularly in local and regional trade shows, conventions or like events in the Territory, and conducting regular local promotional and other marketing efforts for the Product; (c) provide quality product support to resellers, including, but not limited to, providing appropriate installation and application advice and prompt follow-up service and advice to resellers of the Product upon request; (d) provide a support center to resellers, including, but not limited to, a hotline service to answer reseller questions and to receive and track complaints and any reports of claimed errors in the Product; 3 (e) provide quality product technical and sales training to resellers; (f) respond promptly to sales leads or referrals furnished by WGT or by other distributors or dealers of WGT;
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will use its best efforts to develop sales of the Product in the Territory. In furtherance thereof, Distributor may: (a) keep on hand inventory of the Product; (b) establish a program to market the Product, including participating in trade shows; (c) provide product support to resellers; (d) provide a support center to resellers; 3 (e) provide technical and sales training to resellers; (f) respond to sales leads or referrals furnished by WGT or by other distributors or dealers of WGT;
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will' to 'may' and removing the adverb 'aggressively' and adjetives like 'quality' and 'reasonable' the changed text introduces ambiguity and makes the Distributor's obligations optional rather than mandatory. The original text imposes clear obligations on the Distributor to promote the Product actively and meet specific support standards. The modified text significantly weakens these obligations, now creating uncertainty because the Distributor is not strictly required to do those things.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to and in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, WGT hereby appoints Distributor, and Distributor hereby accepts WGT's appointment, as a nonexclusive distributor of the Product to resellers in the Territory during the Term, as long as Distributor makes the Guaranteed Minimum Purchases pursuant to Section 4.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'will' with 'may' and removes adverbs and adjectives, making the Distributor's obligations optional. The contradicted text states that the Distributor accepts the appointment as a distributor 'as long as Distributor makes the Guaranteed Minimum Purchases'. The weakening of obligations in the changed text regarding the distributor's effort to develop sales introduces ambiguity as to whether the distributor is still fulfilling its obligations to maintain the agreement, thus leading to a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor will place orders for the Product from WGT by completing, signing and submitting to WGT a written order for the same, in a form acceptable to WGT, via facsimile, mail or other means. Distributor shall submit such order at least thirty (30) days in advance of the delivery date set forth in each order. All orders will be subject to acceptance by WGT through written acceptance or shipment of the Product subject to the order.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will place orders for the Product from WGT. Distributor may submit orders any time. All orders will be considered accepted by WGT.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that orders must be in writing and submitted 30 days in advance of the delivery date. It also states that acceptance is required. The modified text removes the requirement for written orders and advance notice, and makes order acceptance automatic. This creates a contradiction because the original text establishes specific order procedures that the modified version voids, raising questions about the validity of orders.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, Distributor will make Gross Purchases in an amount at least equal to the cumulative Guaranteed Minimum Purchase amounts through committed orders placed pursuant to paragraph 3.5 and calling for shipment on or before the dates set forth in Exhibit A.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the requirements for written orders and advance notice, and it makes order acceptance automatic. The contradicted text states that the distributor must make gross purchases in an amount equal to the Guaranteed Minimum Purchase amounts through committed orders placed pursuant to paragraph 3.5. Paragraph 3.5, as originally written, requires advanced notice and a written order. Because the modified text eliminates those requirements, it is no longer clear how a 'committed order' is placed under paragraph 3.5, therefore creating a contradiction. This is a strong contradiction because it impacts the fulfillment of minimum purchase obligations, and is an example of Omissions – In Text Contradiction.
